entry.title: Washington Post: 9 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: Fate of eastern Ukraine at stake in Severodonetsk, Zelensky says
  • Who are the Gupta brothers, the family accused of looting South Africa?
  • Hacked Russian radio station broadcasts Ukrainian anthem
  • U.N. seeks deal on food exports as war drives up prices and hurts millions worldwide
  • Ukraine, coping with war losses, adds new polish to military funerals
  • Why a 1970s-style ‘stagflation’ crisis would hit the world’s poor hard
  • Car crashes into Berlin crowd, killing 1; driver is detained
  • Top general details plan to train Ukrainians on rocket artillery
  • Colombia discovers two shipwrecks, releases new images of sunken treasure
  • Song Hae, a South Korean TV host with staying power, dies at 95
  • The scene after a car crashed into a Berlin crowd, killing 1 and injuring several more
  • Greece: Appeals court overturns seizure of Iran tanker's oil
  • Berlin driver's 'confused' statements under investigation
  • Israeli PM Bennett in snap UAE visit amid standoff with Iran
  • UN watchdog says Iran plans to ramp up uranium enrichment
  • Spain evacuates 2,000 people as crews race to check wildfire
  • 10 killed, 3 missing in central China flooding
  • No way out as Iraq's dangerous post-election impasse deepens
  • China and Russia defend North Korea vetoes in first at UN
  • Iran says 2 UN watchdog devices at nuclear site turned off
  • Activists try giant screen to clean up Guatemalan river
  • Social agitators, joyfully Black: The artistic heirs of Gordon Parks
  • Police say brothers — 11 and 14 — broke into gun store, stole 22 weapons
  • Military aircraft carrying five Marines crashes in California
  • 5 percent of young adults identify as trans or nonbinary, survey says
  • AR-15s useful to shoot prairie dogs, GOP leader says, as gun talks intensify
  • Man sues American Airlines, says mistake led to 17-day jail ‘nightmare’
  • DNA ties ‘Pillowcase Rapist’ to six cold cases, officials say
  • Lawyers for migrants say U.S. officials slowed family reunifications
  • Tracking Biden’s nominees to fill the top roles in his administration
  • Post Politics Now: Gun violence victims testify in House; Biden hosting Summit of the Americas
  • Most Americans expect inflation to get worse, Post-Schar School poll finds
  • My co-worker is asking to borrow my clothes
  • Empty wallets, empty tanks: Surging gas prices leave drivers stranded
  • Facebook’s ban on gun sales gives sellers 10 strikes before booting them
  • Help Desk Quick Fix: how to save your precious voice mails
  • Why foundation cracks in your home shouldn’t be ignored
  • Distinguished homes for sale in the D.C. region
  • Manassas Park embraces its youth, diversity and community spirit
  • A $450 Billion Opportunity to Loosen Putin’s Energy Grip
  • The Jan. 6 Committee Should Keep Calling It an Attempted Coup
  • Even 3% Yields Can’t Entice Wary Treasury Investors
  • India’s Central Bank Is Getting Its Way on Rates. But For How Long?
  • Understanding Monkeypox and How Outbreaks Spread
  • How Putin’s Spooking Japan Further Away From Pacifism
  • Meet the Guptas, Symbols of South African Corruption
  • Centrists’ new war on crime is also a war on the left
  • I ran for office full of anger and despair. I felt hope when I dropped out.
  • Please, Democrats, no showhorses at the Jan. 6 hearings
  • Congress must find the courage shown by families affected by gun violence
  • Crime needs to be more than just a gun issue for Biden
  • How NIMBYISM chokes off affordable housing even in Big Sky Country
  • It’s true our Hawaiian Islands are overrun — and not just by chickens
  • A blunt lesson in a Montgomery graduation speech
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • What are the Democrats’ biggest problems? Chat with Perry Bacon Jr.
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Send a letter to the editor
  • Contact the reader representative
  • Submit an op-ed
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: NY Times: 9 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia Is Racing to Cement Its Control in Southern Ukraine
  • Jan. 6 Hearings Will Put Trump at the Center of Plot That Resulted in Capitol Riot
  • Covid and Race
  • As Survivors of Gun Violence Demand Action, House Passes Bill Doomed in the Senate
  • Seeking a Return to Offices, Bosses Lost Leverage
  • The Billionaires Behind a Push to Reinvigorate U.S. Chip-Making
  • Saudi Arabia’s LIV Golf Series Upends Genteel World of Golf
  • Why Farmers Are Getting Priced Out of the Hudson Valley
  • Why Lifeguards Are Scarce This Beach Season
  • Abortion Didn’t Feel Like an Option. Neither Did Motherhood.
  • The Insurrection Didn’t End on Jan. 6. The Hearings Need to Prove That.
  • The Jan. 6 Committee Has Already Blown It
  • Liz Cheney Will Not Tolerate Trump’s Lies
  • Russia and America Are Parting Ways
  • We Need Hope to Combat Violence. That Won’t Arrive Without Action.
  • America’s ‘Psychic Numbing’ to Gun Violence
  • Will Prime Time Undermine or Elevate the Jan. 6 Hearings?
  • The Proud Boys’ Path to Jan. 6
  • Modern Love Podcast: Marriage Classes at Guantánamo
  • Honoring and Celebrating Juneteenth and Pride Month
  • Chinese Pilots Sent a Message. American Allies Said They Went Too Far.
  • Hundreds Have Left N.Y. Public Defender Offices Over Low Pay
  • Biden Administration to Set Rules of the Road for Charging Electric Vehicles
  • Charging Decision Expected in Michigan Police Killing of Black Man
  • Walking May Prevent New Knee Pain for Some, Study Suggests
  • Ukraine Live Updates: ‘The Fate of Our Donbas Is Being Decided,’ Zelensky Says of Sievierodonetsk
  • From the Graveside to the Front, Ukrainians Tell of Grim Endurance
  • Men Missing in Amazon Faced Threats Before They Disappeared
  • Russia Has Filled Ukrainian Water Canal to Crimea
  • At Least 7 Killed in Explosion and Fire in Daegu, South Korea
  • Belgian King Returns Mask to Congo in Landmark Visit
  • In Ukraine, Evacuating the Vulnerable Amid the Terror of War
  • Iran Turns Off U.N. Surveillance Cameras at Nuclear Site
  • Australia’s Baby Steps Toward Severing Ties With the Queen
  • Car Plows Into a School Group in Berlin, Killing 1 and Injuring 14
  • Sewol Ferry Disaster in South Korea Leaves Unhealed Wounds
  • Netanyahu’s Plan to Regain Power in Israel: Vote Against His Views
  • A Radical Collective Takes Over One of the World’s Biggest Art Shows
  • White House Outlines Covid Vaccine Plan for Children Under 5
  • Your Thursday Briefing
  • Your Thursday Briefing: Russia’s Land Bridge
  • A Wider Lens on the MeToo Backlash: Who Pays for Societal Change?
  • As Russia tries to solidify its ‘land bridge,’ Ukraine attempts to push back in the south.
  • Harvey Weinstein Facing Indecent Assault Charges in Britain
  • Angela Merkel Says She ‘Won’t Apologize’ for Her Russia Diplomacy
  • Ryanair Asks Travelers to Prove South African Nationality, in Afrikaans
  • Harvard’s President Will Step Down Next Year
  • How Gender Neutral Language Is Shaping the Fight for Abortion Rights
  • In Uvalde, a Search for Answers: How Could This Happen?
  • At Summit of Americas, Biden Pledges U.S. Help on Latin American Problems
  • Military Plane Carrying 5 Marines Crashes in California, Officials Say
  • How South Dakota Voters Won a Power Struggle With G.O.P. Legislators
  • Brookings Puts Retired Gen. John Allen on Leave Amid Lobbying Inquiry
  • Jan. 6 Inquiry Votes Aren’t Costing G.O.P. Incumbents in Primaries, Yet
  • Oris Buckner, Detective Who Blew Whistle on Police Abuse, Dies at 70
  • House Democrats Dared Republicans to Oppose White Supremacy Measure. They Obliged.
  • White House Struggles to Talk About Inflation, the ‘Problem From Hell’
  • Twitter Said to Agree to Give Elon Musk Access to Stream of Tweets
  • NPR Names Juana Summers as Co-Host of ‘All Things Considered’
  • Trader Joe’s Workers File for Election to Create Company’s First Union
  • How a Crisis Actor Conspiracy Theory Rises in Times of Tragedy
  • Things to Do in Chicago: Food, Museums and More
  • Jump in Mortgage Rates Could Add $100,000 to Housing Costs
  • ECB Prepares for First Interest Rate Increase in a Decade
  • Lawrence D. Ackman, a Cityscape’s Financier, Dies at 83
  • What Texas Gains by Investigating Twitter’s Fake Accounts
  • It’s Easier Than Ever to Travel Abroad Without Cash
  • Credit Suisse Issues Another Profit Warning
  • In meme stock trading fix, S.E.C. to preview new rules.
  • Spirit Delays Frontier Vote, as Bidding War With JetBlue Heats Up
  • Higher Inflation Predicted in Latest O.E.C.D. Outlook
  • Is It Finally Twilight for the Theater’s Sacred Monsters?
  • How ‘Trustless’ Is Bitcoin, Really?
  • Scooters Get a Second Chance
  • Lina Khan, a Big Tech Critic, Tries Answering Her Own Detractors
  • Dave Smith, Whose Synthesizers Shaped Electronic Music, Dies at 72
News from the New York Times

entry.title: JoongAng Daily: 9 Jun, 2022
entry.content:

  • Deregulation and reform matter

  • Balancing the meritocracy

The Korea JoongAng Daily is an English-language daily published by the JoongAng Group, Korea’s leading media group, in association with The New York Times.

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 9 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 9 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-09 11:00AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Japan reopens to visitors on June 10, but Hong Kong travellers may have to delay departure, with visa applications up in the air
  • Credit card company sues wife of former Hong Kong leader CY Leung for failing to pay HK$90,000 bill
  • Hong Kong teenagers arrested, 10.5kg of ketamine seized during crackdown on drug syndicate
  • No chance of reopening border soon with mainland, Lam says, while city logs 674 Covid-19 cases
  • Hong Kong invites global financiers to two-day November summit in much-heralded bid to reclaim city’s spot in world finance
  • 2 jobless Hong Kong men jailed 10 years for causing woman’s death in snatch theft, judge calls crime a ‘cowardly attack’
  • Body of missing Hong Kong hiker, 52, found a day after she was swept away while swimming in country park stream
  • Hong Kong passes long-awaited labour bill to scrap MPF offsetting mechanism, protecting workers’ pensions
  • Early years matters
  • As it happened: ‘No chance of border with mainland China reopening in near future’, Hong Kong’s Carrie Lam says in last Legco session
  • Hong Kong set to double levy on plastic bags, charge producers and suppliers extra for glass beverage bottles in drive to reduce waste
  • Coronavirus: Aji Ichiban co-founder may lower rents for vacant shops after Covid-19 losses forced once-thriving Hong Kong snack chain to shut down
  • Look beyond dollar signs to see Jumbo Floating Restaurant’s true value to Hong Kong
  • Hong Kong’s Fire Services Department readies 2,000 additional officers to handle possible 6th Covid-19 wave, in bid to ease strain on paramedics
  • Hong Kong universities’ international reputation takes a hit in latest world rankings, but result still ‘broadly positive’
  • Ex-worker, parents charged in HK$900,000 bribes-for-kindergarten places case at Hong Kong ESF school
  • Life in Hong Kong’s worst living spaces: from cage homes to subdivided flats
  • Hong Kong police break up another money-laundering syndicate, arresting 16 linked to HK$2.5 billion in suspected crime proceeds
  • Chinese court awards woman US$4,500 compensation for 7 years of housework and child-rearing from ex-husband after divorce
  • China digital currency: e-CNY ‘well positioned’ but still ‘a way to go’ to challenge US dollar dominance
  • Xiaohongshu, dubbed China’s Instagram, tests Web3 waters with investment in US start-up Shil.me
  • Xi tour highlights modern farmland role to boost China’s food security
  • Shanghai moves towards ‘watershed moment’ with Disney resort reopening partially; city reports six new Covid-19 cases
  • Alibaba, Meituan paid the bulk of China’s US$3 billion antitrust fines in 2021, report shows
  • Chinese military jet crash leaves 1 dead, 2 injured and houses on fire in central Hubei province
  • 10 dead, hundreds of thousands displaced as heavy rains lash Hunan, central China
  • Video: Large sinkhole discovered in southern China, the 30th of its kind in the same county
  • China trade: export growth in May boosted by Shanghai reopening, but ‘temporary blip’ to fade amid headwinds
  • Video: Chinese scientists develop robot worm to explore engine pipes
  • Chinese blockchain platform ChainMaker says it has new technology to keep it secure from quantum attacks
  • Man in China seeks divorce from wife of 16 years after paternity tests reveal 3 daughters he raised are not his children
  • ‘It’s tough’: lockdown drags on for millions in Dandong, near North Korea
  • Three US companies sanctioned for sending defence blueprints to China
  • With Xinjiang top of mind, EU lawmakers urge more hardline approach on China human rights
  • China’s positions crucial in crunch talks as WTO chief eyes deals on fishing and vaccines
  • US confrontation or Chinese cooperation? As the Asia-Pacific contemplates its future security, the choice is obvious
  • US mute on China in unveiling ‘ambitious’ Latin America plans at Summit of the Americas
  • China defends its veto of UN Security Council resolution sanctioning North Korea
  • Janet Yellen says Joe Biden’s team looking to ‘reconfigure’ China tariffs
  • Will China’s rail link between Xinjiang, Kyrgyzstan and Uzbekistan change the great power rivalry in Central Asia?
  • China’s FAST telescope detects mystery radio flashes from 3 billion light years away, with energy to rival the sun
  • Taiwan reports record Covid-19 deaths, spike in local cases
  • China and Singapore to resume military exercises, cooperate on military education
  • Philippines billionaire Razon plans massive solar panel facility
  • Sri Lanka president’s brother, Basil Rajapaksa, resigns from parliament
  • How BJP’s Nupur Sharma’s Prophet Mohammed comments pushed India into diplomatic maelstrom
  • Two Chinese men arrested over alleged plot to cheat on Japan university exam
  • North Korea’s record missile flurry could have cost as much as US$650 million
  • Coronavirus: Japan has dropped its outdoor mask mandate, but the message has not filtered through to the public
  • South Korea office building fire kills at least 7, arson suspected
  • Calls grow to boycott Indian goods in Mideast following insults of Prophet Muhammad and Islam by members of Narendra Modi’s BJP
  • Bon Odori Festival: Malaysian royal intervention saves Japan dance festival from Muslim critics
  • Indonesian navy officers asked for US$375,000 to release tanker off its waters near Singapore
  • Japan’s June 10 reopening: tourism sector braces for surge of travel-starved visitors eager to ‘experience, not see’ the country
  • Malaysia 1MDB scandal: Ex-PM Najib Razak’s wife Rosmah Mansor gives up claim on diamond bracelet seized in 2018 raid
  • Malaysia’s tourism sector stretched thin amid deluge of post-Covid travellers from Singapore, Indonesia, India and Europe
  • Japanese man arrested in Indonesia over Covid relief fraud, estimated at US$7.3 million
  • Battle for Sievierodonetsk ‘brutal’, Zelenksy says as Russia pushes Ukrainian troops to city’s outskirts
  • Russia attacking Ukraine food targets to scare world: governor
  • Russia cracks down on critics of its ‘military operation’ in Ukraine
  • New US nuclear-missile submarines hobbled by billions in growing costs and delays
  • WHO warns of ‘real’ risk as monkeypox outbreak tops 1,000 cases
  • Iran switches off nuclear site cameras as IAEA censure adopted
  • Belgium’s king expresses regret, but offers no apology for colonial past in landmark Congo trip
  • US Olympians and dozens of other women sue FBI over botched Larry Nassar sex abuse probe
  • Girl, 11, tells US Congress how she covered herself in blood to survive Texas school shooting
  • Corruption-hit British Virgin Islands agrees to reforms, avoiding UK rule for now
  • Moderna’s Omicron-specific Covid-19 vaccine shows positive results
  • Five steps global policymakers can take to provide supply solutions to stagflation
  • Teacher killed as man drives car into crowd in Germany, others critically injured
  • Russian army base scrambling for Ukraine war supplies, locals and soldiers say
  • UBS expects family offices’ investments in China to cool down amid regulatory crackdown, real estate stress
  • Buffett-backed BYD boosts price ceiling by 33 per cent for stock buy-back plan in vote of confidence on market outlook
  • Why the yuan isn’t a big winner in shift away from US dollar
  • New World Development’s Humansa expands footprint in health and wellness in Greater Bay Area with acquisition and partnership
  • Ant Group’s latest Singapore expansion brings mobile payment apps from Malaysia and South Korea to the city
  • Apple to handle lending itself for new Pay Later service, but keeps partnership with Goldman Sachs for credit card
  • Hang Seng slides as Meituan, JD.com and tech leaders limp while CNOOC advances on oil price outlook
  • Sunac, China’s fourth-largest developer by sales, to delay payment of US$345 million onshore bond due on Monday, sources say
  • Twitter will give Elon Musk ‘firehose’ of raw tweet data in dispute over bots, reports say
  • Alibaba’s cloud services business launches two new data centres in Saudi Arabia to step up its overseas expansion
  • Hedge fund group files complaint against LME over March nickel trading chaos
  • Why it is in US interests to give up its dollar privilege to forge a more neutral financial order
  • Baidu’s Jidu Auto releases virtual concept robocar as intelligent EV unit moves towards mass production in 2023
  • China’s answer to open-source software giant Red Hat says its boss was taken away for disciplinary investigation
  • Nike’s running app to stop service in China next month, following similar moves by Airbnb and Kindle
  • Disney+ K-drama Link: Eat, Love, Kill – Yeo Jin-goo, Moon Ga-young make a compelling pair in engaging fantasy comedy-drama-mystery
  • New art fair Tokyo Gendai will introduce international contemporary artists to a younger generation of Japanese collectors
  • When Thai food meets Sichuan: Hong Kong chef talks spices, mixing the two cuisines and TCM food at new restaurant Sawadee Chef
  • It takes a hardy tourist to visit Sri Lanka amid protests and power cuts
  • How AI, data are transforming healthcare: three key trends that could change the face of medicine
  • Kristen Stewart and fiancée Dylan Meyer are one of Hollywood’s most stylish couples
  • How reading Stephen Covey’s The 7 Habits of Highly Effective People changed the life of NGO Light Be founder Ricky Yu
  • Who are BTS’ celebrity pals in Hollywood? The K-pop idols hung with Coldplay’s Chris Martin after meeting Joe Biden at the White House, and are close with Lizzo, Halsey and Megan Thee Stallion too
  • The rise and fall of Hong Kong’s Jumbo Floating Restaurant: celebrities from Queen Elizabeth to Tom Cruise flocked to the iconic tourist destination – but what will happen to it now?
  • On Her Watch: Why high jewellery and Métiers d’Art timepieces are the ultimate luxury for women, from Chanel’s Mademoiselle Privé collection to Bruguet and Jaquet Droz watches
  • The extravagant lives of India’s disgraced Gupta family: from mansions in Dubai and South Africa, and Lamborghini and Porsche supercars, to ‘big fat Gupta’ weddings, Atul and Rajesh splashed millions
  • Stranger friends: how Millie Bobby Brown and Noah Schnapp became besties IRL, from meeting on the set of Netflix series Stranger Things and red carpet appearances, to making viral TikToks together
  • Meet Austin Butler, the King of Rock in the new Elvis biopic: the former Disney star sings and plays guitar and is dating Kaia Gerber – but it was ex Vanessa Hudgens who said he should play Presley
  • What will Johnny Depp do next? He’s set for a career comeback amid rumours of Beetlejuice 2 and Pirates of the Caribbean film returns, his Dior luxury endorsements and new music with Jeff Beck
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Guardian: 9 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war: western weapons can win Sievierodonetsk battle, governor says; Zelenskiy issues grain warning – live
  • One that got away: how close Liverpool came to buying Brazil’s Rodrygo for €3m
  • Paris police chief says his figure on Liverpool fans’ fake tickets may be wrong
  • How Messy Millennial Woman became TV’s most tedious trope
  • Cost of filling average car with petrol hits £100 in ‘truly dark day’ for drivers – business live
  • Edinburgh International book festival announces new hybrid format
  • Ex-soldiers ‘inspired by psychic to fight in Yemen’ go on trial in Germany
  • Berlin crash driver had history of mental health problems, authorities say
  • Climate policy dragged into culture wars as a ‘delay’ tactic, finds study
  • Please Fix the Road review – turn chaos to calm in this pleasing puzzle
  • Record feats, lawsuit grief and naming retired popes – take the Thursday quiz
  • ‘She could make anything part of a story’ – what Paula Rego chose to paint and why
  • Baby formula shortage continues to distress parents as production resumes
  • Paper Cup by Karen Campbell review – a journey towards healing
  • ‘So gorgeous you’ll shed tears of joy’: readers’ favourite beaches in Greece
  • ‘I’m scared’: Florida faces uncertain abortion future as 15-week ban set to take effect
  • Jack Leach keeps place in unchanged England team for second NZ Test
  • Arizona’s emergency services brace for triple-digit heatwave as deaths mount
  • US House passes gun control bill but it faces defeat in Senate
  • Dom Phillips: editors around world urge Bolsonaro to do more to find missing journalist
  • ‘It was the best day of my life!’ Guardian readers on Abba Voyage
  • 65,000 year-old ‘Swiss Army knife’ proves ancient humans shared knowledge, research says
  • Politics is totally failing to tackle racism – instead it’s sport that’s leading the way | Mihir Bose
  • The Black Sea blockade: mapping the impact of war in Ukraine on the world’s food supply – interactive
  • No easy ride for Biden as Kimmel tells him to ‘start yelling at people’
  • Secret City: behind the untold gay history of DC politics
  • Huge scale and impact of Israeli incursions over Lebanon skies revealed
  • Congress’s January hearings aim to be TV spectacular that ‘blows the roof off’
  • The mayor thinks New York gets ‘special energy’ from crystals. Is he right?
  • Wallabies call on Japan-based trio for upcoming England Tests
  • Fight Night by Miriam Toews review – a war cry for rebellious women
  • ‘It affected a great number of people’: inside the world of shocking military drug experiments
  • The best films of 2022 so far
  • Can workers cram five days worth of work into four days? Mothers already do | Chitra Ramaswamy
  • I took the train to Strasbourg, France – here’s my guide to the city
  • Isn’t it frightening that a lone woman seeing a policeman now feels afraid, not reassured? | Zoe Williams
  • Republican media blitz aims to discredit Capitol attack hearings
  • Shanghai to lock down 2.7 million, a week after easing Covid restrictions
  • Fury at UN human rights chief over ‘whitewash’ of Uyghur repression
  • EU regulators ‘dismissed evidence’ linking glyphosate to rodent tumours
  • ‘I feel like I’ve got the best job in the world!’ – Amy Lennox, the knockout star of Cabaret
  • A smart cabinet would be plotting to get rid of Johnson. This one is neither smart nor brave | Martin Kettle
  • Beetroot juice may aid people with coronary heart disease – study
  • Seven stowaways and a hijacked oil tanker: the strange case of the Nave Andromeda
  • Mexican megachurch leader jailed in US for more than 16 years for child sexual abuse
  • Fight for Sievierodonetsk will decide fate of eastern Ukraine, says Zelenskiy
  • Celtics move within two wins of title after holding off Warriors in Game 3
  • Ukrainian journalist confronts Russia’s Sergei Lavrov with grain theft claim
  • Beneath what’s become the hidden pandemic lie tales of pain and anguish | Paul Daley
  • Sex, laughs and the snip: an audience with Thailand’s ‘Condom King’
  • Twitter set to comply with Elon Musk demand for data on fake accounts
  • Boris Johnson’s focus on housing dismissed as ‘hot air’ amid economic storm
  • Jack Grealish’s Munich cameo showed freedom he feels with England | David Hytner
  • Wout Weghorst heads late winner as Wales slip to defeat by Netherlands
  • Man arrested near Brett Kavanaugh’s home charged with attempted murder
  • Thieves stole Banksy Bataclan door mural with crowbar, French court told
  • Trials of new Covid vaccine raise hopes of once-a-year booster
  • The Phil Mickelson Circus rolls into golf’s breakaway LIV Series | Ewan Murray
  • I’m a Black gun owner. I have mixed feelings about gun control | Akin Olla
  • Warned of ‘massive’ climate-led extinction, a US energy firm funded crisis denial ads
Latest international news, sport and comment from the Guardian

entry.title: Financial Times: 9 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • China digs in for permanent zero-Covid with testing and quarantine regime
  • Runs on Chinese local banks spur fears over health of regional lenders
  • Spac boom dies as wary investors retreat
  • January 6 committee to allege Trump was at centre of US Capitol attack
  • To explore brave new worlds, astronauts need smart new suits
  • Live news updates: Draghi blames Ukraine war for rising inflation ahead of crunch ECB meeting
  • European stocks drop ahead of ECB policy meeting
  • Apple sidelines Goldman and goes in-house for lending service
  • Apple: new financing arm banks on credit
  • Fire at US natural gas plant deals blow to exports
  • Why ending energy imports from Russia remains essential
  • Khan vows ‘muscular’ US antitrust approach on private equity deals
  • SEC chief launches review of ‘uneven’ US equities market
  • Canada’s oil sands: why some of the world’s dirtiest fuel is now in hot demand
  • Who would want to own a hotel now?
  • Investors are anticipating too much tightening from the ECB, says BlackRock
  • Eurozone cheap money era comes to an end
  • Palantir gears up to expand its reach into UK’s NHS
  • Singapore’s wealthy pursue luxury cars even as inflation drives up prices
  • Leftwinger Mélenchon threatens Macron’s hold on France’s parliament
  • South Korea launches investigations into company behind luna crypto crash
  • Brookings places president John Allen on leave amid FBI probe
  • Citigroup tech glitch revealed during markets stress in March 2020
  • Paula Rego, painter, 1935-2022
  • Twitter to share data with Musk as it presses ahead with vote on $44bn deal
  • Hungary’s Orbán loses friends in Poland over stance on Russia sanctions
  • Ambani’s Reliance teams up with Apollo to make binding bid for Boots
  • Britain’s power networks were underprepared for Storm Arwen, regulator says
  • FirstFT: Apple sidelines Goldman with new lending service

  • VOX POPULI: BOJ governor shows he’s out of touch with the ‘mundane world’
The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
